vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1204: Home tab + live session-context briefing with advisory compliance audit hook

plan.title: Home tab + live session-context briefing with advisory compliance audit hook
plan.author: agent:gpt-5.5

narratives.Problem: Panopticon has no canonical landing surface summarizing the live system, and new conversations or agents start cold without current context about running work, recent decisions, workspace status, memory-first expectations, or feature ownership. Operators must jump between Pipeline, Command Deck, tmux sessions, and memory search to answer basic orientation questions.
narratives.Proposal: Add a Home dashboard tab as the default / route, backed by existing PAN-1052 observation/status data and a new feature registry. Add a shared briefing assembler that writes ~/.panopticon/session-context.md from the live read model, exposes the same content through pan briefing, and injects it into Claude Code and Pi sessions at start plus on UserPromptSubmit when the file changes. Add an advisory memory-first compliance audit that detects past-tense trigger prompts, logs compliance.miss observations when pan memory search was not the first tool call, and prepends a soft warning on the next prompt without blocking tool use.

autoDecisions.summary: Home owns / and Pipeline moves to /pipeline while remaining visible in the Operations nav.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue requires Home to become the landing route. Keeping Pipeline at an explicit path preserves the existing view and avoids deleting current operator workflow.

autoDecisions.summary: Reuse existing PAN-1052 store state and components instead of creating a parallel Home data model.
autoDecisions.rationale: MemoryObservation, MemoryStatus, WorkspaceStatusCard, useObservationFeed, time bucket helpers, and Zustand selectors already express most Home data; a duplicate model would drift and add unnecessary pipeline surface area.

autoDecisions.summary: Implement the feature registry as a small local SQLite service under ~/.panopticon/registry/features.sqlite with async access from dashboard code.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD names that location and schema. Dashboard-server rules prohibit blocking synchronous SQLite work in request paths, so the implementation needs a service/worker boundary similar in spirit to memory FTS.

autoDecisions.summary: Compose briefing freshness and advisory compliance warnings through the existing memory hook injection path.
autoDecisions.rationale: The workspace hook script already registers UserPromptSubmit and posts to /api/memory/inject. Extending that response keeps stdout ordering and prompt mutation centralized instead of adding competing hook scripts.

autoDecisions.summary: Compliance v1 is advisory only even though config includes off/advisory/enforcing as a future-facing enum.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD explicitly marks enforcing mode out of scope. Recording the enum now prevents config churn, but implementation and tests must prove advisory mode never blocks tool calls.

autoDecisions.summary: The briefing cross-workspace section should cite registry ownership and memory-search commands, not inline all sibling workspace memory.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD calls for honest framing. Inlining sibling state would be expensive, stale, and misleading; queryable pointers are safer and match the memory-first behavior this issue is trying to reinforce.

autoDecisions.summary: Browser UAT must use an isolated Playwright/browser profile.
autoDecisions.rationale: The planning context explicitly requires isolated browser verification for UI work so results do not depend on another agent's Playwright session or shared browser state.

items.title: Add Home tab route and dashboard shell

narrative.Action: Introduce a Home tab/page in the dashboard frontend, make / resolve to Home, move Pipeline to an explicit /pipeline route, add Home to the Tab union and sidebar navigation, and make the sidebar logo navigate to Home. Preserve /conv/* routing to Command Deck and keep all existing tabs reachable.

subItems.title: src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Header.tsx Tab union includes home without removing any existing tab IDs
src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx maps / to home, /pipeline to pipeline, and /conv/* continues to select command-deck
Sidebar navigation shows Home and Pipeline as distinct destinations, and the Panopticon logo button goes to Home
Home page shell renders loading and empty states without relying on PAN-1052 data being present

items.title: Render Home header summary cards from live read-model state

narrative.Action: Add Home header summary cards showing running agents, paused/troubled gates, recent merges in the last 24 hours, failed verifications needing attention, and daily cost. Derive values from existing Zustand state and dashboard snapshots where available; use honest zero/empty states when no data exists.

subItems.title: Running agent count is derived from AgentSnapshot rows with running/live statuses rather than a hardcoded value
Paused and troubled gate counts include AgentSnapshot.paused and AgentSnapshot.troubled/consecutive failure state
Failed verifications needing attention are derived from ReviewStatusSnapshot verification/test/UAT failure fields or blocker reasons already in the read model
Daily cost summary reuses existing resource/cost state or an existing cost endpoint and renders a clear unavailable state when cost data is absent

items.title: Render time-bucketed Home activity feed from memory observations

narrative.Action: Add a Home activity feed that consumes PAN-1052 observations across workspaces, filters to actionable observations, sorts newest-first, and groups entries into Just Now / Earlier Today / Yesterday / This Week / This Month / Older buckets using the existing time bucket utility unless a more local Home helper is already present.

subItems.title: Feed reads observations from observationsByIssueId and does not fetch or parse transcript JSONL files directly
Entries are grouped into the PRD time buckets and sorted newest-first within each bucket
Feed displays workspace/issue identity, actionStatus headline, summary/narrative, files, and tags when present
Empty feed state explains that observations will appear after PAN-1052 memory extraction creates them

items.title: Render Home workspace list using WorkspaceStatusCard

narrative.Action: Add a Home workspace section that lists active or known workspaces/issues and renders the existing WorkspaceStatusCard for each, using MemoryStatus rollups and recent observations from the store. Clicking a card should open the relevant workspace/Command Deck destination already used elsewhere in the dashboard.

subItems.title: Every workspace/issue with status or observations renders a WorkspaceStatusCard with phase, headline, recent activity, and next steps
WorkspaceStatusCard stats are derived from existing issue/resource/memory data and render zeros when unavailable
Card navigation uses existing app routing and does not introduce a new broken route for workspace details
Fresh installs render a helpful empty workspace state instead of throwing on missing statusByIssueId or observationsByIssueId data

items.title: Add async feature registry storage service

narrative.Action: Create the feature registry contract and storage layer for ~/.panopticon/registry/features.sqlite. Implement the PRD features table with feature_id, feature_name, description, owning_workspace_id, owning_issue_id, owning_agent_id, status, created_at, updated_at, and tags. Dashboard-reachable access must be asynchronous and must not block route handlers.

subItems.title: Registry database is created at ~/.panopticon/registry/features.sqlite with the PRD features table columns and indexes needed for feature/status/issue lookups
Storage API supports list, show by feature, tag/upsert by issue, untag/remove, and ownership updates by workspace/agent state
Dashboard-server consumers use an async service/worker boundary and add no sync filesystem or blocking SQLite calls in route/service paths
Unit tests cover creating the DB, tagging an issue, listing features, showing a feature, and updating ownership/status

narrative.Action: Register a pan registry command group in the CLI with tag, list, and show subcommands backed by the feature registry service. Output should be useful in terminal-only sessions and stable enough for tests.

subItems.title: pan registry tag <issueId> <feature> creates or updates a feature ownership record for the issue
pan registry list displays feature name, owning issue/workspace/agent when known, status, updated time, and tags
pan registry show <feature> displays the full registry record and exits nonzero with a clear error for missing features
CLI tests cover tag/list/show without requiring a real global registry database

items.title: Populate registry from issue creation and workspace transitions

narrative.Action: Wire feature registry population into issue creation/classification and workspace state transitions. The LLM classifier should extract low-cardinality feature tags from issue title/body with a cheap configurable model, while manual CLI tags remain authoritative overrides.

subItems.title: Issue creation path invokes a mockable classifier that extracts feature tags from title and body without blocking user-visible issue creation on failure
Classifier model/provider/cost behavior is configurable and defaults to a low-cost route consistent with the PRD
Workspace/agent lifecycle updates registry ownership fields and status when work starts, pauses/stops, merges, or closes out
Tests use a mocked classifier and verify manual pan registry tag overrides are not overwritten by later classification output

items.title: Render Knowledge Registry section on Home

narrative.Action: Add a Knowledge Registry section to Home that lists features and owning workspace/issue/agent/status from the registry service. The panel should make ownership discoverable without requiring a terminal command.

subItems.title: Home loads registry entries through a safe dashboard API/RPC backed by the async registry service
Registry rows show feature name, owning issue/workspace/agent where known, status, updated time, and tags
Empty registry state points users to pan registry tag <issueId> <feature> and explains automatic classification will populate future entries
Panel errors are localized to the registry card and do not prevent the rest of Home from rendering

narrative.Action: Create a shared briefing module that assembles the live session-context markdown from current workspace identity, PAN-1052 status/observations, registry entries, memory-first triggers, and tool references. The output should follow the PRD sections and be safe to inject as system prompt context.

subItems.title: Assembler output includes # Working Inside Panopticon, What Panopticon Gives You, How to Read What Follows, Current Workspace, Knowledge Registry, Memory-First Triggers, and Tools sections
Current Workspace section detects when CWD is inside a workspace and includes issue/workspace status, age, trajectory, recent observations, and unfinished next steps when available

Cross-workspace/Knowledge Registry section names owning workspace/issue/agent records and honestly tells agents to query sibling state with pan memory search --all-workspaces instead of inlining all sibling memory
Tools section includes pan memory, pan docs query, pan artifacts, pan briefing, pan registry, and pan compliance command references
Unit tests snapshot representative briefing output for current-workspace, no-workspace, empty-registry, and populated-registry cases

narrative.Action: Add dashboard-server lifecycle wiring that regenerates ~/.panopticon/session-context.md whenever relevant read-model state changes, debounced around 500ms. Use the shared briefing assembler and async filesystem writes.

subItems.title: Dashboard server writes ~/.panopticon/session-context.md on startup and after relevant state changes with approximately 500ms debounce
Writer uses fs/promises or Effect FileSystem and adds no writeFileSync/readFileSync/statSync in dashboard-server reachable code
Debounce coalesces rapid event bursts into a single write while still eventually writing the latest state
Tests cover startup write, debounced update, and write failure logging without crashing the dashboard server

narrative.Action: Add a pan briefing CLI command that prints the same markdown content produced for ~/.panopticon/session-context.md, with an option or default behavior suitable for terminal-only sessions.

subItems.title: pan briefing prints briefing markdown from the shared assembler and includes the same top-level sections as session-context.md
Running pan briefing inside a workspace includes that workspace's current context when available
CLI command is registered from src/cli/index.ts or the appropriate commands module and covered by a command test

items.title: Inject briefing file into Claude Code and Pi session starts

narrative.Action: Update Claude Code and Pi harness launch/wrapper paths so new sessions append ~/.panopticon/session-context.md as system prompt context using each harness's supported mechanism. Preserve existing workspace-specific context injection and model routing.

subItems.title: Claude Code launches include --append-system-prompt-file ~/.panopticon/session-context.md or the current supported equivalent without removing workspace context files
Pi launches receive equivalent briefing context through the Pi-supported prompt/context mechanism
Harness changes do not pass explicit --model flags unless an existing caller already does so for a documented reason
Tests verify launch arguments/context payloads include the briefing file for Claude Code and Pi and preserve existing context arguments

narrative.Action: Extend the existing UserPromptSubmit memory injection path so the next prompt receives a <panopticon-briefing-update> block when ~/.panopticon/session-context.md is newer than the session start or last injected briefing mtime. Avoid repeated injection until the file changes again.

subItems.title: SessionStart records enough per-session freshness state to compare briefing mtime against session start and last briefing injection
UserPromptSubmit output preserves the user's prompt and appends exactly one <panopticon-briefing-update> block when the briefing file is newer
After reinjection, the session marker is updated so repeated prompts do not receive the same briefing again unless the file mtime advances
Freshness tests use fake timers or controlled file mtimes and do not rely on real multi-second sleeps

items.title: Add compliance config and trigger matcher

narrative.Action: Add normalized compliance configuration with mode off/advisory/enforcing and default advisory, plus a pure trigger-matching module for memory-first phrases such as 'we recently', 'last session', 'we decided', 'the X fix', and 'remember when'. Enforcing remains out of scope for behavior.

subItems.title: config-yaml normalization exposes compliance.mode with allowed values off, advisory, enforcing and defaults to advisory
Trigger matcher returns matched phrases for past-tense/project-memory prompts and returns empty for unrelated prompts
Matcher tests cover the PRD examples: 'we recently', 'last session', 'we decided', 'the X fix', and 'remember when'
Setting mode to enforcing does not block tool calls in PAN-1204; it is treated as advisory or reported as not-yet-enforced according to the chosen implementation

items.title: Log advisory compliance misses from Stop hook turns

narrative.Action: Extend Stop-hook turn handling so a last user prompt that matches memory-first triggers is audited against the first tool call. If the first tool call is not pan memory search and compliance mode is advisory, write a compliance.miss observation to the PAN-1052 memory store with trigger phrases, first tool call, role, harness, session, and timestamp.

subItems.title: Stop-hook audit detects first tool call from the same turn and recognizes Bash commands beginning with pan memory search as compliant

When trigger phrases match and first tool call is absent or not pan memory search, writeObservation records a compliance.miss observation with triggerPhrase(s), firstToolCall, agentRole, agentHarness, sessionId, and timestamp
Compliance miss logging is skipped when compliance.mode is off and never blocks or fails the original Stop hook processing
Tests cover compliant first search, missed search, no trigger, mode off, and no-tool-call turns

items.title: Prepend next-prompt advisory warning after recent compliance miss

narrative.Action: Extend prompt-time injection so a recent compliance.miss for the current session causes a soft warning to be prepended on the next prompt, then marks that miss as warned so the same warning is not repeated indefinitely.

subItems.title: Next UserPromptSubmit after a current-session compliance.miss prepends: Last turn included a memory-first trigger but the search wasn't called. Try pan memory search first next time.
Warning appears before the original prompt and before appended memory/briefing context blocks so the agent sees it immediately
The same miss is not warned more than once, but a later distinct miss can trigger a new warning
Mode off suppresses advisory warnings even if miss records exist

narrative.Action: Add a pan compliance command group with a status subcommand that reports current compliance mode and recent compliance.miss count from memory observations or the compliance marker store.

subItems.title: pan compliance status displays the normalized compliance mode and a count of recent compliance.miss observations
pan compliance status supports filtering to current project/workspace/session where existing memory CLI filters make that practical
CLI tests cover advisory default, off mode, and nonzero miss count output with mocked observation data

items.title: Add route, unit, and isolated browser verification for Home

narrative.Action: Add final verification coverage that exercises Home as the default landing page, Pipeline at /pipeline, Home sections with stubbed data, and isolated-browser UI behavior. This bead closes the end-to-end acceptance criteria after all functional pieces exist.

subItems.title: Frontend route tests verify / renders Home, /pipeline renders Pipeline, /board still renders Board, and /conv/<name> still selects Command Deck
Component tests render Home with stubbed agents, review statuses, observations, workspace statuses, registry rows, and empty states
Automated verification includes npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test, and dashboard build when dashboard server code changes
Browser UAT uses an isolated Playwright/browser profile and verifies Home loads as the landing route with summary, activity, workspace, and registry sections visible
